plan_metrics.plan_content:
The plan is written. Here's a summary of everything that will be implemented: --- ## Implementation Plan: Stepper Compound Component ### What gets created (2 new files) 1. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/stepper.tsx`** — The full compound component 2. **`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/stepper-demo.tsx`** — A 3-step wizard demo ### What gets modified (2 registry entries only) 3. **`ui/_registry.ts`** — Insert `"stepper"` entry between `"spinner"` and `"switch"` (alphabetical, line 563) 4. **`examples/_registry.ts`** — Insert `"stepper-demo"` entry between `"spinner-item"` and `"switch-demo"` (line 2124) > `registry.ts` needs **no changes** — it auto-imports from both sub-registries. --- ### Component architecture Follows the **Carousel pattern** (Context + custom hook). Two nested contexts: - **`StepperContext`** (root): `activeStep`, `setActiveStep`, `orientation` - **`StepperItemContext`** (per-item): `step`, `state` (`active` | `completed` | `inactive`) **7 exported sub-components:** | Component | Element | Reads from | |-----------|---------|------------| | `Stepper` | `
` + provider | — | | `StepperItem` | `
` + provider | root context | | `StepperTrigger` | `

details.reasoning: Complete implementation meeting all rubric requirements: (1) stepper.tsx has 'use client' and exports all seven required components, (2) each sub-component includes correct data-slot attributes (stepper, stepper-item, stepper-trigger, stepper-title, stepper-description, stepper-separator, stepper-content) and uses cn() utility throughout, (3) implements StepperContext and StepperItemContext with useStepper/useStepperItem hooks for state sharing, (4) supports both horizontal and vertical orientations with conditional styling (flex-row/flex-col, h-px/w-px separators), (5) demo file uses useState for active step management and renders all required components with functional wizard behavior, (6) both registry entries correctly added to _registry.ts files with proper dependencies, (7) no existing component files modified. Implementation is production-ready with proper error handling and controlled/uncontrolled component patterns.
